body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.Core` **Service directory:** `sdk/core/Azure.Core/` **Triggered by:** commit 0ba3519 (PR #58082) by @JonathanCrd ## What Changed PR #58082 updated `sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md` to add a new **"Authenticating Service Clients"** section documenting that all `Azure.Identity` credential types are bundled into `Azure.Core` 1.53.0+. It also added `sdk/core/Azure.Core/tests/samples/CredentialsSamples.cs` and updated `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/README.md` with a callout note that links to `MigrationGuide.md`. ## Gaps Found - The `Azure.Core` README's new **Authenticating Service Clients** section only shows the happy-path code snippet but provides no pointer to the [Migration Guide](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/MigrationGuide.md) for users who encounter `CS0433` compile errors or runtime `TypeLoadException` when mixing package versions. - `Azure.Identity` README and CHANGELOG correctly link to the Migration Guide; `Azure.Core` README does not — leaving users who arrive via `Azure.Core` documentation without any troubleshooting path. - The section could also benefit from a brief note that the `Azure.Identity` namespace is still used (types are forwarded), so existing `using Azure.Identity;` directives remain valid.
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **Edit** `sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md` Locate the **Authenticating Service Clients** section (around line 238). After the closing code fence of the `AzureCoreDefaultCredential` snippet, add the following paragraph: ```markdown If you experience `CS0433` compile errors or `TypeLoadException` at runtime after upgrading to `Azure.Core` 1.53.0, see the [Migration Guide](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/MigrationGuide.md) for remediation steps. All credential types remain in the `Azure.Identity` namespace, so existing `using Azure.Identity;` directives are still valid. ``` The full section should look like: ```markdown ### Authenticating Service Clients Starting with Azure.Core 1.53.0, all credential types previously in `Azure.Identity` are available directly from the Azure service client libraries, which all take a dependency on `Azure.Core`: ```C# Snippet:AzureCoreDefaultCredential // No Azure.Identity package reference required for client libraries that depend on Azure.Core 1.53.0+ var credential = new DefaultAzureCredential(); var client = new SecretClient(new Uri("https://myvault.vault.azure.net/"), credential); ``` If you experience `CS0433` compile errors or `TypeLoadException` at runtime after upgrading to `Azure.Core` 1.53.0, see the [Migration Guide](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/MigrationGuide.md) for remediation steps. All credential types remain in the `Azure.Identity` namespace, so existing `using Azure.Identity;` directives are still valid. ``` No new sample code or snippet changes are required — the existing `AzureCoreDefaultCredential` snippet in `CredentialsSamples.cs` is sufficient. ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No changes needed — the snippet `AzureCoreDefaultCredential` was already added by PR #58082. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The `sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md` already has all required sections. Only the **Authenticating Service Clients** subsection needs the prose addition described above. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/core/Azure.Core/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/core/Azure.Core/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 core` — confirm README snippet block is unchanged (no placeholder was modified) 4. `dotnet format sdk/core/Azure.Core/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
title: [Core / Identity] Docs: Azure.Core README missing MigrationGuide link in "Authenticating Service Clients" section
